mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly reducing present equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.03 during periods of stable unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand expansion, which boosts corporate profit forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce positive returns in >60% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance with p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, which triggers short-term positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >50% of rising INDPRO months, or the signal reverses direction during high-inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 200 while the federal funds rate remains above 4.5 percent, real borrowing costs rise sharply, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >55% of threshold-crossing months, or the signal shows no predictive edge with Rank IC below 0.02 in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >50% of positive payroll months, or the signal Rank IC falls below 0.03 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP and month-over-month increases in retail sales confirm synchronized demand expansion, which elevates equity risk premiums and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative returns in >60% of aligned periods, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant with p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in industrial production and housing starts signal a sharp contraction in supply-side activity and construction demand, which triggers immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 0.5% and HOUST declines > 5% month-over-month
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >50% of triggered instances, or the threshold condition fails to separate return distributions with t-test p-value above 0.05.
